rationale.root_cause: In CWE416_Use_After_Free__malloc_free_char_01_bad(), data is freed (free(data)) and then immediately used via printLine(data), i.e., a use-after-free at lines 34–36. The freed buffer is passed to printf through printLine, causing reads from invalidated memory.
rationale.local_fix: Move the print before the free (or defer free until after use), e.g., call printLine(data) and then free(data) and set data = NULL. This prevents reading the buffer after it has been freed.
rationale.global_fix: Introduce explicit ownership/lifetime management (e.g., an OwnedString wrapper or RAII types like std::string/unique_ptr) so destruction happens at scope end and consumers only get valid views. This makes the object lifetime explicit and prevents premature frees leading to UAF.
